name: Inspiratory time (Ti)
description: Inspiratory time (Ti) — duration of the inspiratory phase of a ventilator breath, in seconds.

en.name: Inspiratory time (Ti)
en.shortDescription: Inspiratory time (Ti) — duration of the inspiratory phase of a ventilator breath, in seconds.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Inspiratory time (Ti) is the duration of the inspiratory phase of a ventilator breath — the time during which gas is delivered to the patient. Together with expiratory time it sets the inspiratory:expiratory (I:E) ratio. In source data it is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp, set or measured by the ventilator. It is recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia for ventilated patients. It is reported in seconds (s).

## Included Concepts

The set lists the SNOMED and LOINC concepts for this duration. All are reported in seconds.

- *4353947 / 250819002 — Inspiratory time (SNOMED)*
- *3043415 / 33435-9 — Inspiratory breath [Time] --on ventilator (LOINC)*
- *36304672 / 76334-2 — Inspiratory time setting Ventilator (LOINC)*

**Note on units**: reported in **seconds** (`8555 / s — second`, UCUM), the recommended unit.

## Mapping Notes

- **Default concept** when not otherwise qualified: *4353947 / 250819002 — Inspiratory time*.
- **Setting vs measured**: map the set value to the LOINC 'setting' concept and the measured value to the measured concept; use the generic SNOMED concept when the source does not distinguish them.
- This is one specific breath-phase duration; inspiratory time, expiratory time and inspiratory pause time are separate concept sets (separate variables).

fr.name: Temps inspiratoire (Ti)
fr.shortDescription: Temps inspiratoire (Ti) — durée de la phase inspiratoire d’un cycle ventilatoire, en secondes.

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Split the former "Breath cycle timing" set into one set per variable (one column each). This set is now Inspiratory time (SNOMED 250819002 + LOINC measured 33435-9 and setting 76334-2). Expiratory time -> set 363, Inspiratory pause time -> set 364.
